verses.2: “In truth I know that this is so; But how can a person be in the right with God?
verses.3: “If one wished to dispute with Him, He could not answer Him once in a thousand times.
verses.4: “Wise in heart and mighty in strength, Who has defied Him without harm?
verses.5: “ It is God who removes the mountains, and they do not know how, When He overturns them in His anger.
verses.6: “ It is He who shakes the earth from its place, And its pillars tremble;
verses.7: Who commands the sun not to shine, And puts a seal on the stars;
verses.8: Who alone stretches out the heavens, And tramples down the waves of the sea;
verses.9: Who makes the Bear, Orion, and the Pleiades, And the constellations of the south.
verses.10: “ It is He who does great things, the unfathomable, And wondrous works without number.
verses.11: “If He were to pass by me, I would not see Him; Were He to move past me, I would not perceive Him.
verses.12: “If He were to snatch away, who could restrain Him? Who could say to Him, ‘What are You doing?’
verses.13: “God will not turn back His anger; Beneath Him the helpers of Rahab cower.
verses.14: “How then can I answer Him, And choose my words before Him?
verses.15: “For though I were right, I could not answer; I would have to implore the mercy of my Judge.
verses.16: “If I called and He answered me, I could not believe that He was listening to my voice.
verses.17: “For He bruises me with a storm And multiplies my wounds without cause.
verses.18: “He will not allow me to get my breath, But He saturates me with bitterness.
verses.19: “If it is a matter of power, behold, He is the strong one! And if it is a matter of justice, who can summon Him?
verses.20: “Though I am righteous, my mouth will condemn me; Though I am guiltless, He will declare me guilty.
verses.21: “I am guiltless; I do not take notice of myself; I reject my life.
verses.22: “It is all one; therefore I say, ‘He destroys the guiltless and the wicked.’
verses.23: “If the whip kills suddenly, He mocks the despair of the innocent.
verses.24: “The earth is handed over to the wicked; He covers the faces of its judges. If it is not He, then who is it?
verses.25: “Now my days are swifter than a runner; They flee away, they see no good.
verses.26: “They slip by like reed boats, Like an eagle that swoops on its prey.
verses.27: “Though I say, ‘I will forget my complaint, I will put my face in order and be cheerful,’
verses.28: I am afraid of all my pains, I know that You will not acquit me.
verses.29: “I am guilty, Why then should I struggle in vain?
verses.30: “If I washed myself with snow, And cleansed my hands with lye,
verses.31: Then You would plunge me into the pit, And my own clothes would loathe me.
verses.32: “For He is not a man, as I am, that I may answer Him— That we may go to court together!
verses.33: “There is no arbitrator between us, Who can place his hand upon us both.
verses.34: “Let Him remove His rod from me, And let not the dread of Him terrify me.
verses.35: “ Then I would speak and not fear Him; But I am not like that in myself.
